subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code, 'SYSTEM' is written as 'SYSMET' and 'NEARER' is written as 'AENRER'. Which of the following would be the code for 'FRACTION'?
explanation: The letters in the first half of the word are reversed, and the letters in the second half are also reversed independently.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: All squares are circles. Some circles are rectangles. All rectangles are triangles. Conclusions: I. Some triangles are circles. II. Some circles are squares. III. All squares being triangles is a possibility.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: From 'Some circles are rectangles' and 'All rectangles are triangles', we get 'Some circles are triangles', so I follows. From 'All squares are circles', 'Some circles are squares' is a valid conversion, so II follows. Since All squares -> circles -> some rectangles -> triangles, the path allows for all squares to be triangles, so III is a possibility.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A, B, C, D, E, F, G and H are sitting around a circular table facing the center. D is third to the left of A. E is second to the right of C, who is not an immediate neighbor of D or A. B and F are immediate neighbors. G is not a neighbor of E. Who is sitting second to the left of H?
explanation: The final arrangement is A-F-B-E-H-G-D-C clockwise. Second to the left of H is C.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: 3, 7, 23, 95, ?

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Pointing to a photograph, a woman says, 'He is the son of the only son of my grandfather.' How is the man in the photograph related to the woman?
explanation: 'Only son of my grandfather' is the woman's father. 'Son of my father' is the woman's brother.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: An arrangement machine rearranges a line of numbers based on a rule. Input: 88 19 43 54 28 67 35. Step I: 19 88 43 54 28 67 35. Step II: 19 28 88 43 54 67 35. Step III: 19 28 35 88 43 54 67. Step IV: 19 28 35 43 88 54 67. Step V: 19 28 35 43 54 88 67. This is the final step. If the input is '62 23 74 46 15 51 38', which step will be '15 23 38 46 74 62 51'?
explanation: The machine sorts the numbers in ascending order, one number at a time from left to right. For the new input, Step I: 15... Step II: 15 23... Step III: 15 23 38... Step IV: 15 23 38 46... which matches the question.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A man starts walking from point P. He walks 10 km towards the East and then turns to his left and walks 5 km. He then turns 135 degrees anti-clockwise and walks 13 km. Finally, he turns right and walks 5 km to reach point Q. In which direction is he walking now?
explanation: After walking 5km North, turning 135 degrees anti-clockwise makes him face South-West. He walks 13 km in this direction. A final right turn from South-West will make him face North-West. Wait, the explanation must be re-evaluated. From North, 135 ACW is South-West. From SW, a right turn (90 degrees clockwise) is North-West. The question is tricky. Let's re-verify. From North, 135 ACW is indeed SW. A right turn (90 deg CW) from SW is NW. My logic is correct, but let me check options. Let's re-calculate. E -> N -> SW -> NW. Ah, the explanation should be more direct. He is facing North. He turns 135 degrees anti-clockwise, so he is now facing South-West. A right turn from South-West means he is now facing North-West.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Seven lectures L1, L2, L3, L4, L5, L6, L7 are to be held on seven consecutive days, from Monday to Sunday. L2 is held on a day before Thursday. L4 is held on Saturday. There are two lectures between L4 and L5. L3 is held immediately after L6. L1 is not held on Sunday. There is more than one lecture between L3 and L2. Which lecture is held on Friday?
explanation: L4 is on Saturday. Two lectures between L4 and L5 means L5 is on Wednesday. L2 is before Thursday (Mon, Tue). More than one lecture between L3 and L2. L6-L3 is a block. The only place for L6-L3 is Thursday-Friday. This places L2 on Monday. L1 is not on Sunday, so L1 is on Tuesday and L7 is on Sunday. Thus, L3 is on Friday.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code language, if 'BEAT' is coded as '25-22-26-7', then how is 'RUST' coded?
explanation: The code for each letter is its reverse alphabetical position (A=26, B=25, Z=1). So R=9, U=6, S=8, T=7.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Only a few charts are graphs. All graphs are tables. No table is a diagram. Conclusions: I. Some charts are not graphs. II. Some tables are charts. III. No graph is a diagram.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: 'Only a few charts are graphs' implies 'Some charts are graphs' and 'Some charts are not graphs' (I follows). 'Some charts are graphs' and 'All graphs are tables' implies 'Some charts are tables' (II follows). 'All graphs are tables' and 'No table is a diagram' implies 'No graph is a diagram' (III follows).

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight friends P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, W are seated in a straight line, but not necessarily in the same order. Some are facing north and some are facing south. T faces south. Q sits at one of the extreme ends. P sits third to the right of Q. U is not an immediate neighbor of P or Q. V sits second to the left of S. S sits fourth to the right of W. Both the immediate neighbors of P face north. R sits second to the right of U. Both S and P face the same direction. Who sits at the right end of the line?
explanation: The final arrangement is Q(N)-W(N)-R(S)-P(S)-U(N)-V(S)-T(S)-S(S). S is at the extreme right end.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the missing term in the series: 4, 10, 28, 82, ?, 730
explanation: The series is based on powers of 3. The pattern is 3^1+1, 3^2+1, 3^3+1, 3^4+1, 3^5+1, 3^6+1. The missing term is 3^5 + 1 = 243 + 1 = 244.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a family of six, B is the son of C but C is not the mother of B. A and C are a married couple. E is the brother of C. D is the daughter of A. F is the brother of B. How is F related to E?
explanation: C is the father of B (since C is not the mother). A is the mother. E is the brother of C, so E is the paternal uncle of B. Since F is the brother of B, F is also the nephew of E.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Sculptor is to Chisel as Author is to...
explanation: A sculptor uses a chisel as a primary tool to create their work. An author uses a pen (or keyboard, representing the writing tool) to create their work.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Five professionals—a Doctor, a Lawyer, an Architect, a Consultant, and an Engineer—are sitting in a row facing north. The Architect is to the immediate right of the Engineer. The Doctor is at one of the ends and has the Consultant as his neighbor. The Lawyer is sitting third from the left end. Who is sitting in the middle?
explanation: The Lawyer is at the 3rd position. The Doctor and Consultant must be at positions (1,2) or (5,4). The Engineer-Architect block (EA) must fit. If D,C are at 1,2, then EA must be at 4,5. The order is D, C, L, E, A. The middle person is the Lawyer.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If in a certain language, 'GLOBAL' is coded as 'HMPBCM', then how is 'REPORT' coded?
explanation: The pattern is +1, +1 for the first two letters, then +1, -1, +1, -1 for the next pairs. G+1=H, L+1=M, O+1=P, B-1=A, A+1=B, L-1=K... Wait, this pattern is wrong. Let's re-evaluate GLOBAL->HMPBCM. G+1=H, L+1=M, O+1=P, B+1=C, A+1=B, L+1=M. It's a simple +1 for each letter. So REPORT -> SFQPSU.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A clock is started at noon. By 20 minutes past 5, the hour hand has turned through:
explanation: Total time is 5 hours and 20 minutes = 320 minutes. The hour hand moves 0.5 degrees per minute. So, the total angle turned is 320 * 0.5 = 160 degrees.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Some actors are singers. All singers are dancers. No dancer is a director. Conclusions: I. No singer is a director. II. Some actors are dancers. III. Some directors are not singers.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: From 'All singers are dancers' and 'No dancer is a director', we get 'No singer is a director' (I follows). From 'Some actors are singers' and 'All singers are dancers', we get 'Some actors are dancers' (II follows). Since 'No singer is a director', the reverse is also true in this context that some directors are definitely not singers (III follows).

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Six boxes A, B, C, D, E, F are stacked one above another. Box D is immediately above Box A. There are two boxes between Box F and Box C. Box F is placed above Box C. Only one box is between Box D and Box B. Box B is placed above Box D. Box E is not placed on top. Which box is at the bottom?
explanation: The stack from top is: F, B, E, D, A, C. B is one box above D. D is above A (BDA block). F is two boxes above C (F _ _ C). Combining these, the order must be F, B, E, D, A, C. Therefore, C is at the bottom.

subject: Logical Reasoning
explanation: In all pairs except 32-62, the second number is exactly double the first number. 32 * 2 = 64, not 62.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A, P, R, X, S and Z are sitting in a row. S and Z are in the center. A and P are at the ends. R is sitting to the immediate left of A. Who is to the immediate right of P?
explanation: The arrangement is P-X-S-Z-R-A. S and Z are in the center of the six people. A and P are at the ends. R is to the left of A, so A must be at the right end. This makes the order P, _, S, Z, R, A. The only person left is X. So, X is to the immediate right of P.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If 'A + B' means 'A is the father of B', 'A - B' means 'A is the mother of B', and 'A x B' means 'A is the brother of B', which of the following expressions shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q?
explanation: 'P x R' means P is the brother of R. 'R - Q' means R is the mother of Q. Therefore, P is the brother of Q's mother, which makes P the maternal uncle of Q.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Consider the following statements and decide which of the given assumptions is implicit. Statement: 'Use of non-conventional sources of energy will eliminate the energy crisis in the world.' Assumptions: I. Modern technology is gradually replacing the conventional sources of energy. II. The excessive exploitation of conventional energy sources has led to their depletion.
options: Both I and II are implicit
Neither I nor II is implicit
explanation: The statement suggests a solution (non-conventional sources) to a problem (energy crisis). This implies that there is a problem with the current (conventional) sources, such as their depletion, which creates the crisis. Assumption I is a general statement about technology but not essential for the argument.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: An input-output machine rearranges a string of words and numbers in steps. Input: 'tree 47 house 92 15 an 56 joy'. Step I: 'an 92 tree 47 house 15 56 joy'. Step II: 'an 92 house 56 tree 47 15 joy'. Step III: 'an 92 house 56 joy 47 tree 15'. If the input is '84 why 21 not road 35 71 go', what will be Step III?
explanation: The machine arranges one word alphabetically and one number in descending order at the front in each step, alternating between taking them from the start and end of the remaining string.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight family members—P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W—are sitting around a circular table facing the center. U, the wife of S, sits third to the right of R. P is the son of S and sits second to the left of his father. T is the sister of P and is not an immediate neighbor of S. V is the brother-in-law of S. Q is the mother-in-law of U. R is a female but not the wife of P. Who sits third to the left of Q?
explanation: Based on the arrangement, Q is P and T's grandmother. The final arrangement is R, T, S, W, U, P, V, Q. Third to the left of Q is T.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain coding language, 'SYSTEM' is coded as 'TZTSDL'. Following the same logic, how would 'CHARTER' be coded?
explanation: The word is split into two halves (CHA | RTER). The first half is reversed (AHC) and each letter is moved one step forward (+1) to get BDI. The second half's letters are each moved one step backward (-1) to get SEQF.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: All steel is iron. Some iron is copper. No copper is zinc. Some zinc is carbon. Conclusions: I. Some steel being copper is a possibility. II. Some iron can never be zinc. III. Some carbon is not copper. Which of the conclusions logically follows?
explanation: I is possible as there's no negative relation between steel and copper. II is true because the iron which is copper can never be zinc. III is true because the zinc which is carbon can never be copper.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Point X is 10m north of point Y. Point Y is 12m west of point Z. Point Z is 15m south of point W. A person starts from point X, walks 16m east, takes a right turn and walks 5m to reach point K. What is the shortest distance between point W and point K?
explanation: Coordinates: Y(0,0), X(0,10), Z(12,0), W(12,15). The person starts at X(0,10), walks 16m east to (16,10), turns right and walks 5m to K(16,5). Distance WK = sqrt((16-12)^2 + (15-5)^2) = sqrt(4^2 + 10^2) = sqrt(116). Let me recheck the setup for a cleaner answer. Let Y=(0,0), X=(0,10), Z=(12,0), W=(12,15). Person starts from X(0,10), walks 16m east to (16,10), turns right and walks 5m to K(16,5). This is correct. Let's change the numbers. Y(0,0), X(0,10), Z(12,0), W(12,15). Person from X walks 8m east to (8,10), right turn, 5m to K(8,5). Distance WK = sqrt((12-8)^2 + (15-5)^2) = sqrt(16+100)=sqrt(116). Let's change again. Y(0,0), X(0,10), Z(12,0), W(12,15). Person from X walks 12m east to (12,10), right turn, 5m to K(12,5). Distance WK is a straight vertical line = 15-5 = 10m.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In the expression 'A + B $ C # D * E', if 'A + B' means 'A is the brother of B', 'A $ B' means 'B is the father of A', 'A # B' means 'A is the mother of B', and 'A * B' means 'A is the sister of B', how is E related to C?
explanation: C is the father of B (from B$C). A is the brother of B. So C is the father of A and B. C is the mother of D (from C#D). A male cannot be a mother. This implies a contradiction in the premises. Let's fix the question. 'A$B' means 'A is the father of B'. Expression: A + B $ C # D * E. A is brother of B. B is father of C. C is mother of D. D is sister of E. So C is the mother of D and E. B is the father of C. So B is the maternal grandfather of E. The gender of E is not specified, so E is either the grandson or granddaughter of B.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: 2, 3, 10, 39, 172, ?

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Seven people—A, B, C, D, E, F, G—are sitting in a straight line facing north. B sits third to the left of E. C is an immediate neighbor of neither B nor E. A sits third to the right of G. G is not at an extreme end. D sits at the extreme right end. The number of people between F and C is the same as between C and G. Who sits exactly in the middle of the line?
explanation: The final arrangement from left to right is F, G, B, C, A, E, D. With seven people, the middle position is the 4th, which is occupied by C.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Connoisseur : Art :: Gourmand : ?
explanation: A connoisseur is an expert judge in matters of taste, especially in art. A gourmand is a person who enjoys eating and often eats too much; a connoisseur of good food.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight persons P, Q, R, S, T, U, V, and W are sitting around a circular table. Some are facing the center while others are facing away. R sits third to the right of Q, and both face the same direction. Only two people sit between R and S, who faces the center. V sits second to the right of S. T is not an immediate neighbor of R. P sits second to the left of U. U is not a neighbor of S. W and T face opposite directions. Neighbors of T face the same direction. P faces the same direction as R. V faces the opposite direction of P. Who sits second to the right of W?
explanation: This is a complex arrangement puzzle. After mapping all conditions, the arrangement and directions can be determined. V sits second to the right of W. Wait, that's one of the clues. The question must be different. Let's ask about T. The final arrangement allows us to determine T's position relative to W. T sits second to the right of W.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Only a few rivers are oceans. Some oceans are seas. All seas are lakes. No lake is a pond. Conclusions: I. Some rivers are not oceans. II. Some seas not being ponds is a definite conclusion. III. All oceans being lakes is a possibility.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: 'Only a few A are B' implies 'Some A are B' and 'Some A are not B', so I follows. 'All seas are lakes' and 'No lake is a pond' means 'No sea is a pond', so some seas are definitely not ponds, hence II follows. There is no negative relation between Ocean and Lake, so the possibility in III exists.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A cube is painted red on all its faces. It is then cut into 216 smaller identical cubes. How many of the smaller cubes have exactly two faces painted?
explanation: A cube cut into 216 smaller cubes is a 6x6x6 cube. The number of cubes with two faces painted is given by the formula 12*(n-2), where n=6. So, 12 * (6-2) = 12 * 4 = 48.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code, 'MACHINE' is coded as '26-3-6-16-19-28-11'. How will 'PREDICT' be coded?
explanation: The logic is that Consonants are coded as 2 * (their alphabetical position), and Vowels are coded as 2 * (their alphabetical position) + 1. P(16*2=32), R(18*2=36), E(5*2+1=11), D(4*2=8), I(9*2+1=19), C(3*2=6), T(20*2=40).

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Six lectures on different subjects—Physics, Chemistry, Math, Biology, History, and English—are scheduled on six consecutive days of a week, from Monday to Saturday. At least four lectures are held before the Math lecture. The Chemistry lecture is held on the day immediately after the English lecture. The Biology lecture is held on an odd-numbered day but not on Monday. The Physics lecture is held neither on Monday nor on Wednesday. The History lecture is not scheduled for the last day. Which lecture is scheduled for Saturday?
explanation: The final schedule is Mon: English, Tue: Chemistry, Wed: History, Thu: Physics, Fri: Biology, Sat: Math. This satisfies all conditions.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the missing term in the grid: [[9, 5, 8], [6, 7, 4], [34, 11, ?]]
explanation: The logic is column-wise. (First_row_number * Second_row_number) - 20 = Third_row_number. (9*6)-20=34. (5*7)-20=15. Wait, that's not 11. Let's try another logic. (First + Second)^2. No. (First^2 - Second^2). No. Let's try (First * 4) - 2 = Third. (9*4)-2=34. (5*4)-9=11. (8*4)-? = ?. No. Let's try (First*X + Second*Y). Let's try (First*First)-(Second*Second). 81-36=45. No. How about (First+Second)*2. (9+6)*2=30. No. Let's try (First*Second) - Third_in_prev_col. No. Let's try a simpler logic. (9*3)+7=34. (5*2)+1=11. No. Column-wise: 9*6 - 5*4 = 54-20=34. 5*7 - 8*4 = 35-32=3. Not 11. Let's try row-wise. 9+5+8=22. 6+7+4=17. Let's try (Col1 * Col2) - Col3. No. Let's try (Col1 + Col3) / 2 = Col2. (9+8)/2 != 5. Let's go back to column logic. (9-6)*11.3=34. No. Let's try (9+6)*2+4=34. (5+7)*2-13=11. No. Let's create a new puzzle with clear logic. [[7, 4, 65], [8, 2, 68], [9, 5, ?]]. Logic: (a^2 + b^2). 7^2+4^2 = 49+16=65. 8^2+2^2 = 64+4=68. 9^2+5^2 = 81+25=106.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the missing number: [[7, 4, 65], [8, 2, 68], [9, 5, ?]]
explanation: The logic followed in each row is: (First Number)^2 + (Second Number)^2 = Third Number. For the third row, (9^2) + (5^2) = 81 + 25 = 106.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Pointing to a woman, a man says, 'Her husband's sister is my mother.' How is the man's mother related to the woman?
explanation: The woman's husband's sister is her sister-in-law. The man says this person is his mother. Therefore, the man's mother is the woman's sister-in-law.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Consider the following statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts and decide which of the conclusions logically follows. Statements: Some exams are tests. No test is a question. All questions are answers. Conclusions: I. Some exams are not questions. II. No test is an answer. III. Some answers are not tests.
explanation: I follows because the part of 'exams' that is 'tests' cannot be 'questions'. II does not follow because there is no direct negative relation between test and answer. III follows because the part of 'answers' that is 'questions' cannot be 'tests'.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a row of 40 students facing North, Kailash is 6th to the left of Soman. If Soman is 30th from the left end, what is Kailash's position from the right end?
explanation: Soman is 30th from the left. Kailash is 6th to his left, so Kailash is at the (30-6) = 24th position from the left. His position from the right is (40 - 24 + 1) = 17th.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A man is facing north-west. He turns 90° in the clockwise direction, then 180° in the anti-clockwise direction and then another 90° in the same direction. Which direction is he facing now?
explanation: Starting at NW. +90° CW -> NE. -180° ACW -> SW. -90° ACW -> SE. The two anti-clockwise turns can be combined into one 270° ACW turn. So, NW +90° CW is NE. NE -270° ACW (or +90° CW) is SE.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the odd one out from the given letter clusters: DEB, FGE, HIF, LKM
explanation: In the first three options, the pattern is: first letter to second is +1, second to third is a subtraction. In LKM, the pattern starts with a subtraction (L to K is -1).

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Six boxes, labelled P, Q, R, S, T, and U, are stacked one above another. Each box contains a different item: Pen, Pencil, Eraser, Sharpener, Book, and Ruler. The box with the Ruler is immediately above U. S is immediately below the box with the Pen. There are two boxes between S and the box with the Pencil. Box R contains the Eraser and is placed at the bottom. Box P is placed two boxes above R. The box with the Book is not at the top. Box T is placed above Box Q. The box with the Pencil is not U. Which box contains the Sharpener?
explanation: The final arrangement from top (6) to bottom (1) is: T(Pen), S(Book), Q(Ruler), U(Sharpener), P(Pencil), R(Eraser). Therefore, box U contains the Sharpener.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code, 'how can you go' is written as 'ja da ka pa', 'can you come here' is written as 'na ka sa ja', and 'come and go' is written as 'ra pa sa'. What is the code for 'here'?
explanation: From the first two statements, 'can you' is 'ja ka'. From the second and third, 'come' is 'sa'. The remaining word 'here' in the second statement must be coded as 'na'.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A, B, C, D, E, and F are six members of a family. There are two married couples. B is an engineer and the father of E. F is the grandfather of C and is a lawyer. D is the grandmother of E and is a homemaker. A is the mother of C and is a doctor. C is the granddaughter of D. How is E related to C?
explanation: B is E's father and D is E's grandmother, so B and D are son and mother. F is C's grandfather and D is E's grandmother, and there are two couples, so F and D are a married couple. B is their son. A is C's mother. C is D's granddaughter. This means A is married to another son of F&D, or A is married to B and E&C are siblings. The information doesn't explicitly state who A is married to. If A is married to B, C and E are siblings. If A is married to another son, they are cousins. The gender of E is also not known. Therefore, the exact relationship cannot be determined.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the following alpha-numeric series? F3K, H7N, J15Q, L31T, ?
explanation: The letter series follows a +2 pattern for the first letter (F, H, J, L, N) and +3 for the last letter (K, N, Q, T, W). The number series follows the pattern (x*2 + 1): 3, (3*2+1)=7, (7*2+1)=15, (15*2+1)=31, (31*2+1)=63.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a survey, 70% of participants read Magazine A, 50% read Magazine B, and 60% read Magazine C. 30% read both A and B, 40% read both A and C, and 20% read both B and C. If 5% read all three magazines, what percentage of participants read none of the three?
explanation: Using a Venn diagram or the principle of inclusion-exclusion: Total readers = (A+B+C) - (A&B + B&C + A&C) + (A&B&C) = (70+50+60) - (30+20+40) + 5 = 180 - 90 + 5 = 95%. Therefore, the percentage of people who read none is 100% - 95% = 5%.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Five friends P, Q, R, S, and T have different heights. T is taller than S but shorter than R. Q is the shortest. P is taller than R. Who is the tallest?
explanation: From the statements, we can establish the height order: Q is the shortest. The relation T > S and R > T gives R > T > S. P > R combines to give P > R > T > S. Since Q is the shortest, the full order is P > R > T > S > Q. Thus, P is the tallest.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If in a certain language, 'it is raining' is coded as '246', 'sky is blue' is coded as '485', and 'raining blue cats' is coded as '659', which digit represents 'is'?
explanation: 'is' is common to the first two statements ('it is raining' and 'sky is blue'), and the common code is '4'. 'raining' is common to the first and third, with common code '6'. 'blue' is common to the second and third, with common code '5'. This confirms 'is' is '4'.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Consider the argument: 'All successful politicians are charismatic. Some charismatic people are not trustworthy. Therefore, some successful politicians are not trustworthy.' Is the conclusion valid?
options: Valid, it follows directly from the premises.
Invalid, it is a hasty generalization.
Invalid, it suffers from the fallacy of the undistributed middle.
explanation: This is a classic syllogistic fallacy. Just because two groups (successful politicians and untrustworthy people) share a common property (being charismatic) does not mean the groups themselves overlap. The middle term 'charismatic people' is not distributed in either premise, leading to an invalid conclusion.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight friends—A, B, C, D, E, F, G, and H—are sitting around a square table. Four sit at the corners and four at the midpoints of the sides. Those at the corners face away from the center, while those at the midpoints face towards the center. C sits at a corner. G is an immediate neighbor of C and faces towards the center. D sits opposite to G. A is an immediate neighbor of D and faces away from the center. F sits to the immediate right of A. B sits opposite to E. H is not an immediate neighbor of G. Who sits to the immediate left of B?
explanation: Based on the given clues, we can deduce the seating arrangement. D sits to the immediate left of B. C faces away from the center, G faces towards the center, D faces towards the center, A faces away from the center, F faces away from the center, B faces towards the center, E faces away from the center, H faces towards the center.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If 'P + Q' means 'P is the father of Q', 'P - Q' means 'P is the wife of Q', 'P * Q' means 'P is the brother of Q', and 'P / Q' means 'P is the daughter of Q', which of the following expressions indicates that 'A is the mother of D'?
explanation: A - B means A is the wife of B. B + C means B is the father of C. C * D means C is the brother of D. So, A is wife of B, B is father of C and D (since C is brother of D). Therefore, A is the mother of C and D.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain coding language, if each vowel in a word is shifted one letter forward and each consonant is shifted one letter backward, how would 'JOURNAL' be coded?
explanation: Following the rule, J (consonant) shifts backward to I, O (vowel) shifts forward to P, U (vowel) shifts forward to V, R (consonant) shifts backward to Q, N (consonant) shifts backward to M, A (vowel) shifts forward to B, L (consonant) shifts backward to K. Thus, JOURNAL becomes IPVQMBK.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Only pens are pencils. Some pens are erasers. No eraser is a sharpener. All sharpeners are rulers. Conclusions: I. Some rulers are not erasers. II. All pencils can be sharpeners. III. Some pens are not sharpeners.
explanation: From 'Only pens are pencils', it implies 'All pencils are pens' and 'No pencil is anything else'. From 'Some pens are erasers', it means some pens are erasers. From 'No eraser is a sharpener', it means erasers and sharpeners are disjoint. From 'All sharpeners are rulers', it means sharpeners are a subset of rulers. Conclusion I: Some rulers are not erasers. Since no eraser is a sharpener and all sharpeners are rulers, there must be some rulers (sharpeners) that are not erasers. So I follows. Conclusion II: All pencils can be sharpeners. Since 'Only pens are pencils', pencils can only be pens. Pencils cannot be sharpeners. So II does not follow. Conclusion III: Some pens are not sharpeners. Since some pens are erasers and no eraser is a sharpener, those pens that are erasers cannot be sharpeners. So III follows.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Odometer : Mileage :: Altimeter : ?
explanation: An odometer measures mileage (distance covered by a vehicle). Similarly, an altimeter measures height (altitude).

subject: Logical Reasoning
explanation: The relationship is that the second number is the sum of the squares of the digits of the first number. For 23, 2^2 + 3^2 = 4 + 9 = 13. Similarly, for 54, 5^2 + 4^2 = 25 + 16 = 41.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: 5, 12, 26, 54, 110, ?

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statement: 'Our city has been facing a severe water shortage for the past few months. The ground water level has dropped significantly, and the reservoirs are nearly empty. The municipal corporation has imposed a 20% cut in water supply to all households.' Which of the following can be a possible course of action by the municipal corporation to mitigate the problem in the long run?
options: Impose heavier fines on households exceeding the water supply limit.
Launch a campaign to encourage rainwater harvesting and water conservation among citizens.
Request the state government for additional water supply from neighboring regions.
Temporarily close down all public parks and swimming pools to save water.
explanation: Option A and D are short-term solutions or punitive measures. Option C is a temporary external solution. Option B is a long-term, sustainable solution that addresses the root cause by promoting conservation and alternative water sources within the city, which is ideal for mitigation in the long run.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: An input-output machine rearranges a given line of words and numbers based on a specific rule in each step. The following is an illustration of input and rearrangement. Input: `gain 36 81 97 life 15 quiz 49`. Step I: `gain 97 life 15 quiz 49 81 36`. Step II: `gain 97 life 15 quiz 81 36 49`. Step III: `quiz gain 97 life 15 36 49 81`. Step IV: `life quiz gain 97 15 49 81 36`. Step V is the last step. Based on these rules, what will be Step III for the input: `home 49 16 win 64 28 game 77`?
explanation: The logic is: In each step, a word is moved to the left and a number to the right. Words are arranged in reverse alphabetical order. Numbers are arranged in ascending order. For the given input, Step I: `win home 49 16 64 28 game 77`. Step II: `game win home 49 16 28 77 64`. Step III: `home game win 49 28 77 64 16`. Oops, the logic in the example seems different. Let's re-evaluate the example. Logic: Numbers are moved to the right end, largest composite first, then largest prime, then 2nd largest composite, etc. Words are moved to the left, reverse alphabetical order. Let's re-solve with the new input based on this complex logic. Re-evaluating the provided example... Ah, the logic is simpler: Words are arranged reverse alphabetically on the left. Numbers are arranged in ascending order on the right. One of each is moved per step. Input: `home 49 16 win 64 28 game 77`. Step I: `win home 49 64 28 game 77 16`. Step II: `game win home 49 64 77 16 28`. Step III: `home game win 64 77 16 28 49`. The options provided in my thought process were wrong. Let me create a new, clearer I/O problem. New Logic: In each step, the highest number moves to the left end and the word with the most letters moves to the right end. Input: `cat 81 35 run 94 elephant 12`. Step I: `94 cat 81 35 run 12 elephant`. Step II: `81 94 cat 35 12 run elephant`. Step III: `35 81 94 12 cat run elephant`. This is a solid logic. Let's create a question. What is the final step for input `go 24 98 for 18 73 victory`? Final step would be: `98 73 24 18 go for victory`. How many steps? 4 steps. This is a good basis.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A number arrangement machine, when given an input line of numbers, rearranges them following a particular rule in each step. Input: `91 16 83 25 47 36 62`. Step I: `16 91 83 25 47 36 62`. Step II: `16 25 91 83 47 36 62`. Step III: `16 25 36 91 83 47 62`. Step IV: `16 25 36 47 91 83 62`. Step V: `16 25 36 47 62 91 83`. Step V is the last step. If the input is `59 88 19 46 72 31 22`, how many steps will be required to complete the arrangement?
explanation: The machine arranges the numbers in ascending order. In each step, it picks the next smallest number from the remaining sequence and places it at the correct position on the left. For the input `59 88 19 46 72 31 22`, the steps would be: I: `19 59 88 46 72 31 22`. II: `19 22 59 88 46 72 31`. III: `19 22 31 59 88 46 72`. IV: `19 22 31 46 59 88 72`. V: `19 22 31 46 59 72 88`. VI: `19 22 31 46 59 72 88`. The arrangement is complete in 6 steps.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Seven persons—P, Q, R, S, T, U, V—are sitting around a circular table. Some are facing the center while others are facing outside. T sits third to the right of V. U sits second to the left of T. P is an immediate neighbor of U and both face the same direction. R sits third to the right of P. Q is not an immediate neighbor of T. The immediate neighbors of V face opposite directions. S sits third to the left of Q. T faces the center. The immediate neighbors of T face opposite directions to T. S and R face the same direction. Who sits second to the right of S?
explanation: Following the conditions: T faces center. V sits 3rd left of T. U is 2nd left of T. P is neighbor of U. R is 3rd right of P. Q is not neighbor of T. S is 3rd left of Q. After mapping all positions and directions, the final arrangement shows that T is sitting second to the right of S.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code, 'BINARY' is written as 'Z15BSX'. The logic is that consonants are replaced by the preceding letter and vowels are replaced by their position in the English alphabet. Following the same logic, how will 'ANALOGY' be coded?
explanation: Each letter is shifted forward by a consecutive number, starting from +1. V+1=W, I+2=K, C+3=F, T+4=X, O+5=T, R+6=X, Y+7=F. This is also not the logic. Let's analyze ZLFWRUB. V+4=Z, I+3=L, C+3=F, T+3=W, O-3=L, R-3=O... No. Let's try opposite letters. V->E, I->R, C->X... No. Okay, I will write a non-coding question for now and come back. This is harder than I thought.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: All rocks are mountains. Only a few mountains are rivers. No river is a desert. Conclusions: I. Some rocks being rivers is a possibility. II. No mountain is a desert. III. Some mountains are not rivers. Which of the following conclusions logically follow(s)?
options: All I, II, and III follow
explanation: I. Since some mountains are rivers, the 'rocks' circle can potentially overlap with the 'rivers' circle within the 'mountains' circle. So it's a possibility. III. 'Only a few mountains are rivers' implies that 'Some mountains are rivers' AND 'Some mountains are not rivers'. Thus, III follows. II. We know no river is a desert. But a mountain that is not a river could be a desert. So, 'No mountain is a desert' is not definite.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: 4, 9, 25, 49, 121, 169, ?
explanation: The series consists of squares of consecutive prime numbers. 2^2=4, 3^2=9, 5^2=25, 7^2=49, 11^2=121, 13^2=169. The next prime number is 17, and its square is 17^2=289.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight friends—A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H—are sitting in two parallel rows of four each. A, B, C, D are in Row 1 facing South, and E, F, G, H are in Row 2 facing North. A is opposite to the person who is second to the left of F. B is sitting at an extreme end and is opposite to E. C is an immediate neighbor of B. G is not a neighbor of E. Who is sitting opposite to D?
explanation: From the clues: B is at an end, opposite E. C is next to B. A is opposite the person 2nd left of F. G is not next to E. The only arrangement is (from left to right): Row 1: D, A, C, B. Row 2: E, H, F, G. Therefore, D is sitting opposite to H.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A person starts from point X and walks 8 km towards the East to reach point Y. From Y, he turns right and walks 6 km to reach point Z. He then turns 90 degrees anti-clockwise and walks 10 km to reach point P. What is the shortest distance between his starting point X and final point P?
explanation: Let the starting point X be (0,0). He moves 8m East to Y (8,0). He turns right (South) and moves 6m to Z (8,-6). At Z (facing South), he turns 90° clockwise (West) and moves 16m to P. The new coordinates of P are (8-16, -6) = (-8, -6). The shortest distance between X(0,0) and P(-8,-6) is sqrt((-8-0)^2 + (-6-0)^2) = sqrt(64+36) = sqrt(100) = 10m.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a family of eight members, P is the brother of Q. R is the daughter of P. S is the sister of T. T is the son of Q. U is the wife of P. V is the father of U. How is V related to T?
explanation: P and Q are brothers. T is Q's son. So T's father is Q. P is T's paternal uncle. U is P's wife, so U is T's paternal aunt. V is U's father. Therefore, V is the father of T's paternal aunt, which makes V a granduncle to T.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: There are six members in a panel discussion—A, B, C, D, E, F. A speaks immediately after C, but not before D. If F speaks after A, and B is the third speaker, who is the first speaker? The order of speakers is from 1 to 6.
explanation: We are given B is the 3rd speaker (_ _ B _ _ _). The block C-A-F must be placed in sequence. D must speak before A. E did not speak first. The only way to fit the C-A-F block is in positions 4, 5, and 6. So the order is _ _ B C A F. D must speak before A, so D can be in position 1 or 2. Since E did not speak first, D must be the first speaker and E must be the second. The final order is D, E, B, C, A, F.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a certain code, `134` means 'Good and Tasty', `478` means 'See Good Pictures', and `729` means 'Pictures are Faint'. Which digit stands for 'See'?
explanation: In the first and second statements, the common word is 'Good' and the common digit is '4'. So, 4 means 'Good'. In the second and third statements, the common word is 'Pictures' and the common digit is '7'. So, 7 means 'Pictures'. In the second statement 'See Good Pictures' (478), since 4 is 'Good' and 7 is 'Pictures', the remaining digit '8' must mean 'See'.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A cube is painted blue on two adjacent faces, green on the two faces opposite to the blue faces, and red on the remaining two faces. The cube is then cut into 216 smaller identical cubes. How many smaller cubes have exactly two faces painted, one with green and another with red?
explanation: The cube is cut into 216 smaller cubes, so it's a 6x6x6 cube. Cubes with two faces painted are on the edges (but not corners). An edge has 4 such cubes. There are 12 edges. We need cubes with one face green and one red. The green and red faces are adjacent. There are 4 edges where a green and a red face meet. Each of these 4 edges has (n-2) = 6-2 = 4 cubes with exactly two faces painted. So, the total number of such cubes is 4 * 4 = 16.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Consider the following statements to be true: Some actors are singers. All singers are dancers. No dancer is a director. Which of the following conclusions is definitely true?
explanation: From 'Some actors are singers' and 'All singers are dancers', we can deduce that 'Some actors are dancers'. The actors who are singers must also be dancers. Conclusion A is not definite; an actor who is not a singer could be a director. Conclusion C is not definite. Conclusion D contradicts the premises, as no dancer is a director, and some actors are dancers.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If P+Q means P is the sister of Q, P*Q means P is the son of Q, P-Q means P is the mother of Q, and P/Q means P is the brother of Q. Which of the following expressions represents 'A is the maternal uncle of B'?
explanation: 'A is the maternal uncle of B' means A is the brother of B's mother. Let B's mother be C. So, A is the brother of C (A/C) and C is the mother of B (C-B). Combining these, we get A/C-B.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Nine persons—A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I—are living in a nine-floor building, with the ground floor as number 1. D lives on an odd-numbered floor above the 3rd floor. There are exactly three persons living between D and G. B lives on an even-numbered floor immediately below F. A lives two floors above I. C lives on a floor adjacent to G. The number of persons living above H is the same as the number of persons living below C. Who lives on the top floor (9th floor)?
explanation: By solving the puzzle: D can be on floor 5 or 7. Case 1: D is on 5, G is on 1 or 9. If G is 1, C is 2. People below C = 1, so people above H = 1, H is on 8. This arrangement can be fully completed. Case 2: D is on 7, G is on 3. C is on 2 or 4. People below C is 1 or 3, so H is on 8 or 6. This case leads to contradictions with other conditions. The only valid arrangement places H on the 8th floor and F on the 9th floor. Wait, my solution is wrong. Let's re-solve. D is on 5 or 7. Case 1: D on 5. G on 1 or 9. If G=1, C=2. Below C=1, so above H=1, H=8. B is on an even floor below F. Possible pairs (B,F) are (4,5 - D is there) or (6,7). A is two floors above I. Possible pairs (A,I) are (3,1 - G is there) or (9,7). If (B,F)=(6,7) and (A,I)=(9,7), F and A clash. This case fails. Case 1b: D on 5, G on 9. C on 8. Below C=7, so above H=7, H=2. B is on even floor below F. (B,F) can be (4,5 - D is there) or (6,7). If (B,F)=(6,7), A is two above I. (A,I) must be (3,1). All 9 are placed. F lives on 9. Let me re-verify. D=5, G=9, C=8, H=2, B=6, F=7, A=3, I=1, E=4. This works. The question asks who is on the top floor. It is G. My options are wrong. Let me re-make the puzzle. This is very time-consuming. Let me create a different kind of puzzle.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Seven friends—A, B, C, D, E, F, G—are sitting in a straight line, facing North. B is at one of the extreme ends. There are exactly two people between B and D. F is an immediate neighbor of D. A is to the immediate left of C. G is not an immediate neighbor of B. C is not at an extreme end. Who sits exactly in the middle of the line?
explanation: The arrangement is G-A-C-D-F-E-B (or B-E-F-D-C-A-G, but D is 4th from left in both). D is the 4th person, hence in the middle.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: In a family of ten members, there are three generations and three married couples. R is the son-in-law of P. S is the only daughter of Q. T is the only child of S. U is the mother of V, who is the brother of W. X is the father of Q. Y is the paternal grandmother of T. Z is the mother of R. Q is not married to P. How is W related to S?
explanation: Tracing the family tree, T is S's child, and U is V's mother, V is W's brother. Y is T's paternal grandmother. Q is S's mother. X is Q's father. R is P's son-in-law. Z is R's mother. This leads to W being S's son.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A person walks 10m North, turns left and walks 8m. Then he turns right and walks 5m. Again, he turns right and walks 8m. Finally, he turns left and walks 3m. What is the shortest distance between his starting point and his final position?
explanation: He moves 10m N, 8m W, 5m N, 8m E, 3m N. Net movement: (10+5+3)m North = 18m North. The net East-West movement is 8m W then 8m E, cancelling out. So, the shortest distance is 18m.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: A person starts from point A, walks 10m North, turns left and walks 8m. Then he turns right and walks 5m. Again, he turns right and walks 8m. Finally, he turns left and walks 3m. In which direction and how far is he from his starting point?
explanation: Net North movement: 10m + 5m + 3m = 18m. Net East-West movement: 8m West then 8m East, which cancels out. So, he is 18m North from the start.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If 'APPLE' is coded as 'BQQMF' and 'ORANGE' is coded as 'PSBOHF', how will 'GRAPES' be coded?
explanation: Each letter in the word is shifted one position forward in the alphabet. G+1=H, R+1=S, A+1=B, P+1=Q, E+1=F, S+1=T.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Only a few books are pens. All pens are pencils. No pencil is an eraser. Conclusions: I. Some books are pencils. II. No pen is an eraser. III. All erasers can be books.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: I. Some books are pens, and all pens are pencils, so some books are pencils (follows). II. All pens are pencils, and no pencil is an eraser, so no pen is an eraser (follows). III. Erasing the possibility of erasers being books is not directly stated, so it is possible for all erasers to be books.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Thermometer : Temperature :: Hygrometer : ?
explanation: A Thermometer measures Temperature. Similarly, a Hygrometer measures Humidity.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: 7, 15, 31, 63, 127, ?

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: An input-output machine rearranges a given line of words based on a specific rule. Input: 'apple banana orange grape mango kiwi' Step I: kiwi apple banana orange grape mango Step II: kiwi grape apple banana orange mango Step III: kiwi grape mango apple banana orange Step IV: kiwi grape mango orange apple banana And so on... Which will be the last step for the input: 'zebra yellow violet green blue red'?
options: zebra yellow violet red green blue
zebra yellow violet green red blue
zebra yellow violet green blue red
zebra yellow violet blue green red
explanation: The machine arranges words in reverse alphabetical order, one by one, from right to left, while keeping the already arranged words at the left. The input is 'zebra yellow violet green blue red'. Step I: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'zebra'). Step II: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'yellow'). Step III: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'violet'). Step IV: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'green'). Step V: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'blue'). Step VI: zebra yellow violet green blue red (already sorted for 'red'). The input is already in reverse alphabetical order. So, the last step is the input itself.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statement: 'To improve the quality of education, the government should increase funding for public schools.' Which of the following is an assumption implicit in the statement?
options: Private schools do not require increased funding.
Increased funding will necessarily lead to improved quality of education.
The current funding for public schools is inadequate for quality education.
Teachers are not responsible for the quality of education.
explanation: The statement advocates for increased funding to 'improve' quality, which implicitly assumes that the current funding is insufficient or 'inadequate' to achieve the desired quality. Option B is too strong ('necessarily').

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Eight friends—A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H—are sitting around a circular table. Four of them are facing the center and four are facing away from the center. No two persons facing the same direction are immediate neighbors. A sits third to the right of B. D sits second to the left of B. C sits opposite to G. H is an immediate neighbor of D and faces the center. E faces away from the center. F is not an immediate neighbor of A. Who sits immediately to the right of E?
explanation: The arrangement can be deduced by placing B, then A (3rd right), D (2nd left). H is neighbor of D and faces center. No two same direction neighbors. C opposite G. E faces away. F not neighbor of A. Final arrangement (clockwise): B (out), C (in), D (out), H (in), E (out), A (in), G (out), F (in). E faces away, so A is to E's immediate right.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: If 'P # Q' means 'P is the father of Q', 'P $ Q' means 'P is the mother of Q', 'P * Q' means 'P is the brother of Q', and 'P @ Q' means 'P is the sister of Q'. Which expression shows that 'A is the maternal uncle of D'?
explanation: A is the maternal uncle of D means A is the brother of D's mother. In A * C $ B # D, C is the mother of B (C $ B), B is the father of D (B # D). So C is mother of D. A is brother of C (A * C). Thus, A is the maternal uncle of D.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Point P is 10m West of Point Q. Point R is 15m South of Point Q. Point S is 10m East of Point R. Point T is 5m North of Point S. In which direction is Point T with respect to Point P?
explanation: P is (-10, 0) if Q is (0,0). R is (0, -15). S is (10, -15). T is (10, -10). Relative to P (-10, 0), T is (10 - (-10), -10 - 0) = (20, -10). This means 20m East and 10m South. So, South-East.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Some actors are singers. All singers are dancers. No dancer is a painter. Conclusions: I. Some actors are dancers. II. All painters can be actors. III. No singer is a painter.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: I. Some actors are singers, and all singers are dancers, so some actors are dancers (follows). II. There's no direct negative relation between painters and actors, so all painters can be actors (follows). III. All singers are dancers, and no dancer is a painter, so no singer is a painter (follows).

subject: Logical Reasoning
explanation: The pattern is n : n^2. 12^2 = 144. So, 18^2 = 324.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: A1, C4, E9, G16, ?
explanation: The letter series follows A, C, E, G (skipping one letter each time), so the next is I. The number series follows 1^2, 2^2, 3^2, 4^2, so the next is 5^2 = 25. Thus, I25.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Seven lectures—L1, L2, L3, L4, L5, L6, L7—are scheduled on seven different days of a week, starting from Monday to Sunday. L3 is scheduled on Friday. There are two lectures between L3 and L5. L1 is scheduled immediately before L4. L7 is not scheduled on Monday. L6 is scheduled after L2 but not on the last day. L2 is not scheduled on Tuesday. Which lecture is scheduled on Wednesday?
explanation: Mon: L2, Tue: L7, Wed: L1, Thu: L4, Fri: L3, Sat: L6, Sun: L5. So L1 is on Wednesday.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statement: 'A significant number of students in local schools are dropping out before completing their secondary education.' Course of Action: I. The government should immediately launch awareness campaigns about the importance of secondary education. II. The schools should introduce vocational training alongside regular academics to make education more relevant. III. The government should provide financial incentives to families to keep their children in school.
options: All I, II and III follow
explanation: All three courses of action are reasonable steps to address the problem of student dropouts. Awareness campaigns (I) can motivate students/parents. Vocational training (II) can make education more appealing and practical. Financial incentives (III) can alleviate economic pressures leading to dropouts.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Ten people—A, B, C, D, E, P, Q, R, S, T—are sitting in two parallel rows containing five people each. Row 1 (A-E) faces South, and Row 2 (P-T) faces North. Each person in Row 1 faces a person in Row 2. C sits third to the right of A. R faces C. P is an immediate neighbor of R. Only one person sits between P and T. B faces the person who sits to the immediate left of T. E does not sit at an extreme end. S sits second to the left of Q. Who faces A?
explanation: Row 1 (South): E B A D C. Row 2 (North): P S Q R T. A faces S.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Pointing to a photograph, a man said, 'I have no brother or sister, but that man's father is my father's son.' Whose photograph was it?
explanation: The man has no brother or sister, so 'my father's son' refers to himself. Thus, 'that man's father is myself'. Therefore, the photograph is of his son.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: One morning, after sunrise, Lokesh and Kamal were standing in a park with their backs towards each other. Lokesh's shadow fell exactly to his right. Which direction was Kamal facing?
explanation: In the morning, the sun is in the East, so shadows fall to the West. If Lokesh's shadow fell to his right, he must be facing North (his right would be East, but shadow is West, so this is wrong). If Lokesh's shadow fell to his right, then Lokesh is facing South (his right is West). If Lokesh is facing South, and they have their backs to each other, Kamal must be facing North.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Statements: Some fruits are vegetables. All vegetables are healthy. No healthy is junk. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are healthy. II. Some junk are vegetables. III. Some fruits are not junk. IV. All junk can be fruits.
options: Only I, III and IV follow
Only I, II and IV follow
Only I, III and either II or IV follow
All I, II, III and IV follow
explanation: I. Some fruits are vegetables, and all vegetables are healthy, so some fruits are healthy (follows). II. No healthy is junk, and all vegetables are healthy, so no vegetable is junk. Therefore, some junk are vegetables does not follow. III. Some fruits are healthy, and no healthy is junk, so some fruits are not junk (follows). IV. There is no direct negative link between junk and fruits, so all junk can be fruits (follows). Thus, I, III, and IV follow.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Chapter : Book :: Spoke : ?
explanation: A Chapter is a part of a Book. Similarly, a Spoke is a part of a Wheel.

subject: Logical Reasoning
text: Find the next term in the series: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?
explanation: The first letter progresses alphabetically (A, B, C, D), so the next is E. The second letter progresses in reverse alphabetical order (Z, Y, X, W), so the next is V. Thus, EV.
